commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a Computer Use-specific Codex MCP elicitation bridge, enables MCP elicitation delegation when Computer Use is configured, passes the configured server name into the bridge, and adds focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: a same-thread, same-turn form/object elicitation from the configured Computer Use server without `_meta.codex_approval_kind` falls through the current main bridge and is declined by the app-server default. I did not run a live macOS Computer Use reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs human maintainer handling because the remaining blockers are protected-label approval-boundary review and contributor live proof, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(codex): bridge computer use elicitations
target.body:
## Summary

- Computer Use MCP app-approval elicitations now route through the existing app-server approval bridge instead of falling through the plugin-resolution path as unhandled.
- The Codex app-server enables MCP elicitation delegation when Computer Use is enabled and passes the configured Computer Use MCP server name into the bridge.
- Focused tests cover Computer Use server-name scoping, form/schema requirements, empty-schema responses, persistent approval metadata, and the surrounding run-attempt wiring.

## Motivation

Computer Use is an `openai-bundled` MCP server, not a normal app entry in `pluginAppPolicyContext`. Its app-approval elicitation therefore missed the existing plugin-policy matching path and could be returned as `not_plugin`/unhandled even though the request needed to reach the approval bridge.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Tests

## Scope

- [x] Codex app-server / Computer Use elicitation bridge
- [x] Codex app-server run-attempt approval-policy wiring

## Root Cause

`resolvePluginElicitation` only recognizes app-backed connector/plugin metadata. Computer Use app approval requests come from the configured Computer Use MCP server and can have no app-policy entry, so they need a narrow server-name-scoped bridge after plugin matching declines to handle the request. The app-server also needs `mcp_elicitations` enabled whenever Computer Use is enabled so those elicitation requests are delegated back to OpenClaw.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Computer Use app approval elicitations from the configured MCP server are forwarded to `plugin.approval.request` / `plugin.approval.waitDecision` and mapped back into MCP elicitation responses.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on branch `codex/computer-use-elicitation-bridge`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode auto`.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 224 passed (224)` on the final cleaned diff. Targeted oxfmt and oxlint passed, `git diff --check` exited 0, and autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Observed result after fix: Computer Use elicitations are bridged only when the request comes from the configured Computer Use MCP server, uses form mode, and has an object schema. Other servers and unsupported Computer Use-shaped requests remain unhandled.

What was not tested: The live dev-profile flow that opens Apple Notes through Computer Use was not rerun during this cleanup turn.

## Regression Test Plan

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts` covers Computer Use elicitation matching and response mapping.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` covers enabling MCP elicitation delegation and passing the configured Computer Use MCP server name into the bridge.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Computer Use app approval prompts can be accepted through the existing approval bridge instead of being silently declined or ignored by the Codex app-server relay.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

The bridge remains scoped to the configured Computer Use MCP server and still requires an approval decision before Computer Use proceeds.
